Willughbeia eriophora, commonly known as the "beaked willughbeia," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The beaked willughbeia has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Willughbeia eriophora include alkaloids, such as willughbein, eriophorin, and other indole alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Willughbeia eriophora is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its use and efficacy require further scientific investigation and standardization.
